schema.appDescription: Calculate monthly pension income from a retirement corpus using the systematic withdrawal approach. Find sustainable withdrawal rate to last through retirement.

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the input fields for the Pension Income Calculator.

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time as you type.

howToSteps.name: Explore breakdown
howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown to see numbers over time.

howToSteps.name: Compare scenarios
howToSteps.text: Adjust inputs to compare scenarios and optimise your decision.

faq.q: How much monthly pension can ₹1 crore corpus give?
faq.a: ₹1 crore corpus at 7% return for 25 years: sustainable monthly withdrawal is approximately ₹70,000/month. If you only need ₹50,000/month, the corpus can last indefinitely (corpus earns more than withdrawal). ₹70,000/month at 7% for 25 years depletes the corpus at exactly 25 years.

faq.q: What is the difference between annuity and SWP for pension?
faq.a: Annuity (from insurance company): guaranteed income for life, fixed rate, no market risk, income taxable, no liquidity. SWP from mutual fund: market-linked, may vary, corpus remains accessible, more flexible, can increase/decrease withdrawal. SWP is more flexible but carries market risk. Diversify between both.

faq.q: Should I use debt funds or equity for post-retirement portfolio?
faq.a: A common allocation: 40% equity, 60% debt at retirement, shifting to 30/70 by age 70. Equity provides growth to beat inflation; debt provides stability for near-term withdrawals. Bucket strategy: 1–2 years expenses in liquid/ultra-short funds, 3–7 years in debt funds, rest in equity.
No. Calculations run in your browser.
Yes. Works on all modern smartphones.

examples.heading: ₹1 crore corpus — 25 years at 7%
examples.text: Monthly withdrawal: ₹70,645. Annual: ₹8.48 lakh. Total withdrawn over 25 years: ₹2.12 crore. Total returns earned from corpus: ₹1.12 crore. The corpus grows with returns but depletes through withdrawals, reaching zero at exactly year 25.

examples.heading: ₹2 crore corpus — sustainable forever
examples.text: ₹2 crore at 7%: monthly income ≈ ₹1.17 lakh. Annual return ≈ ₹14 lakh, spending ≈ ₹14 lakh — corpus stays intact. This is the 'dividend' or perpetual model where you live off investment returns without depleting principal.
